.ddx/attachments/ddx-bffb23ae/events.jsonl:9:{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead ddx-bffb23ae (Story 12 B3) — no_changes rationale
========================================================

Status: AC7 already satisfied on main; no further code change needed in
this attempt.

What is done
------------
A prior attempt landed commit efbed403 ("feat(agent): add prompt_sha to
manifest + prompt-size CI fixture [ddx-bffb23ae]") which is an ancestor
of HEAD/main in this worktree. Verified with `git merge-base HEAD
efbed403` → efbed403. That commit delivers every AC7 deliverable:

1. prompt_sha in manifest.json for every attempt
   - Field defined: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:177-181
     (`PromptSHA string \`json:"prompt_sha,omitempty"\``)
   - Populated on write: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1127
     (`PromptSHA: promptSHA(promptContent)`)
   - Helper: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1467-1470
     (`promptSHA` returns hex sha256 of the rendered prompt bytes)
   - Regression test: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go
     :230-298 asserts manifest.prompt_sha is a 64-char hex digest and
     equals sha256 of the prompt.md bytes on disk.

2. Render-size CI fixture emitting word/byte counts per (variant,
   contextBudget)
   - Test: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_promptsize_test.go
     (`TestPromptSizeReport`) renders both variants
     (claude harness, agent harness) at contextBudget = "" (full) and
     "minimal", logs the table to test output, writes a tab-separated
     human report and a JSON sibling, and asserts minimal < full bytes
     per variant to catch cheap-tier regressions.
   - Determinism guard: `TestPromptSHA_DeterministicAcrossRenders`
     asserts the helper is stable across renders and differs across
     variants.

3. CI surfaces the report as an artifact
   - .github/workflows/ci.yml:201-214 runs the fixture with
     `DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT=${{ github.workspace }}/prompt-size-report
     .txt` and uploads `prompt-size-report.{txt,json,log}` as the
     `prompt-size-report` artifact.

Verification run in this worktree
---------------------------------
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run
'TestPromptSizeReport|TestPromptSHA|ExecuteBeadArtifacts' -count=1` →
ok (0.012s).

Why the bead was re-queued
--------------------------
The notes section on this bead reads "REVIEW:BLOCK — Diff contains only
this attempt's own execution evidence ... The shown manifest.json itself
lacks a prompt_sha field, contradicting AC7." That review fired against
a *different* attempt whose diff happened to contain only the evidence
files (manifest.json/result.json) for that attempt's own execution
bundle. The actual implementation landed on a separate commit
(efbed403) which is now on main. The recorded manifest.json the
reviewer inspected was generated by an *earlier* execution that ran
before efbed403 was merged, so it predates the field — the manifest.json
emitted by the current binary does include prompt_sha.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------
Nothing for AC7. If the reviewer still doubts the field is being
written, point them at the fresh manifest.json in this attempt's
evidence directory (.ddx/executions/20260502T121152-24b051ff/
manifest.json) and the unit test
TestExecuteBeadArtifacts_ManifestPromptSHA in execute_bead_artifacts_
test.go which asserts prompt_sha is present and matches sha256 of
prompt.md.

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:120:{"acceptance":"`ddx agent execute-bead` resolves governing references for both doc-ID and repo-relative path-style `spec-id` values when the target file exists in the execution snapshot; the generated prompt/manifest include the resolved path reference instead of the `No governing references` fallback; and deterministic tests cover a bead whose `spec-id` is a repo-relative path such as `workflows/README.md`.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"cee85e6565224b30b15f689fa7daac8ad5e4a12b","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:18:51.632271275Z","description":"<context-digest>
Alpha validation run from /Users/erik/Projects/helix using `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` against a live HELIX bead whose `spec-id` is the repo-relative path `workflows/README.md`. Evidence includes `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/prompt.md`, `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json`, and `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go`.
</context-digest>

`ddx agent execute-bead` prompt synthesis failed to resolve a live path-style `spec-id` into governing references, so the generated execution prompt told the agent there were no governing references even though the referenced file exists in the execution snapshot.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, inspect bead `helix-fef22846`; it carries `spec-id=workflows/README.md`.
2. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` or `ddx agent execute-bead helix-fef22846 --json`.
3. Open `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/prompt.md`.
4. Observe the prompt includes `- spec-id: workflows/README.md` but the `## Governing References` section says `No governing references were resolved from the current execution snapshot.`

## Observed gap
`executeBeadResolveGoverningRefs()` builds the doc graph and looks up only by graph document ID. Path-like `spec-id` values that point to real files in the repo are dropped instead of being resolved as governing references.

## Why this matters
This is exactly the context the execution prompt tells the agent to read before changing code or docs. When DDx drops an existing repo-relative path reference, agent execution becomes less grounded and the artifact bundle misstates the available governing context.","events_attachment":"ddx-1f434484/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-1f434484","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","area:agent","area:docs","area:cli"],"priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Resolve path-style spec-id values in execute-bead governing references","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:12:11.237799965Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:161:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, I expect the Sessions page to reflect reality — if I ran `ddx work` today, today's sessions appear within seconds. And I expect the feed not to silently break again the next time the agent execution path is refactored.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Session-index source of truth.** A single writer (invoked from `service.Execute` or a thin wrapper that every production agent-execution path calls) appends one line per completed agent invocation to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`. Fields are pointer-only: id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath (relative), nativeLogRef (relative). No prompt or response bodies embedded.

2. **Single-call-site guarantee.** Unit test enumerates every production code path that executes an agent (execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, replay if still present) and asserts each appends exactly one index row. If a new path is added later without wiring this up, the test fails.

3. **Visibility restored.** GraphQL `sessions` query reads `sessions.index.jsonl`. Running `ddx work` (or any agent-execution path) against a fixture project causes the new session to appear in a GraphQL query within 2 seconds of completion. E2E test demonstrates this.

4. **Body lazy-load.** A new resolver `session(id: ID!) { prompt response stderr }` loads the heavy fields on demand from the execution bundle (`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/`) or the agent-log path, without reading every session in the index.

5. **Legacy migration.** A one-shot command (e.g. `ddx agent log reindex`) seeds the index from the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (397 MB corpus on the current repo), preserving historical sessions. Integration test uses a fixture `sessions.jsonl` and asserts the index has one row per legacy entry with all pointer fields populated.

6. **Deprecated-file behavior.** After migration, the legacy `sessions.jsonl` is not read by the server. `ddx agent usage` and related CLI commands (`cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:197,246,348`, `cli/cmd/status.go:179`) are updated to read from the index too, verified by their existing tests.

7. **Perf sanity.** The GraphQL `sessions` list resolves in ≤100ms p95 on a fixture with 10,000 index entries; current behavior times out or OOMs. Benchmark captured in bead notes as baseline + after-fix.

8. **UI note.** If there is a gap in the index (e.g. the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap on the current repo, assuming backfill from executions/agent-logs is not feasible), the Sessions page surfaces a single-line banner "No sessions recorded between X and Y" so the gap is not silently invisible. If backfill is feasible, do it instead and skip the banner.

9. **Playwright e2e.**
   - Seeds a fixture project with `.ddx/executions/` bundles from both before and after a simulated gap.
   - Runs the migration command.
   - Opens `/sessions`, asserts the most recent session timestamp matches the latest bundle, asserts a clicked row lazy-loads prompt/response from the bundle rather than from an embedded payload.
   - Asserts the feed updates live (within 2s) after a new simulated session is written.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"d733f7f20828708c5179f0ceff9462eaa34654eb","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:22:41.990684692Z","description":"## Observed

The Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.

## Root cause

The Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.

The only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:

- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`
- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`
- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`

Production execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.

## Secondary issue (same fix should address)

`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).

## Proposed direction

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.","events_attachment":"ddx-2ceb02fa/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model "minimax/minimax-m2.7" for harness "gemini"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-010","id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","feat-006","perf","observability"],"notes":"## 2026-04-22 — User-directed amendment: shard the index

Per user feedback: a single `sessions.index.jsonl` just replays the same problem at smaller scale. The aggregate needs to be split.

### Revised storage shape

- Directory: `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/` (per-project, under each registered project's `.ddx`).
- Inside: **monthly shards** named `sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`.
- Each shard is pointer-only, same fields as the previously-proposed index (id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath, nativeLogRef).
- Writer resolves the target shard from `time.Now().UTC()` at append time and creates the shard file lazily on first write of the month.
- Heavy bodies (prompt, response, stderr) stay where they already live — `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` and agent-log streams — and are lazy-loaded by the `session(id:)` resolver.

### Why monthly, not daily / size-based / SQLite

- **Daily** — produces tens of thousands of files over the project lifetime; ReadDir becomes the cost.
- **Size-based** — requires coordinating rotation with the append; race-prone and hard to test.
- **SQLite** — overkill for first pass; revisit if a single monthly shard grows past tens of MB.
- **Monthly** — ≤12 shards/year, human-readable filenames, date-range queries map trivially to shard selection, bounded shard size in practice.

### Reader behavior

- Default Sessions query loads the **current + previous shard** (covers "recent activity" for any current date).
- Date-range queries map the range to a shard set and read only those.
- Pagination beyond the default window triggers older-shard loads on demand.
- No shard-coordination lock needed: each month's file is append-only and only the current-month shard is actively written.

### Legacy migration

- `ddx agent log reindex` reads the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` once and splits by each entry's `timestamp` field into the appropriate monthly shards under `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/`.
- After migration, the legacy file is renamed `sessions.jsonl.legacy` (not deleted) and never read again by the server. Hand-removal is the user's call.
- Idempotent: re-running the command merges without duplicating (index row dedup by `id`).

### Revised acceptance criteria (supersedes criteria 1, 5, 6, 7 on original description)

1. **Sharded index writer.** Single shared call site (from `service.Execute` or equivalent wrapper) appends one line to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, computing the shard from `time.Now().UTC()`. Test: write across a simulated month boundary and assert two shards exist with the correct split.

5. **Legacy migration shards too.** `ddx agent log reindex` splits `sessions.jsonl` into monthly shards by entry timestamp. Integration test uses a fixture with entries across 3 distinct months and asserts 3 shards are produced with correct contents and no duplicates on re-run.

6. **Server reads the shard directory.** `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` reads `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/*.jsonl`, respects any date-range arg, and loads only the shards that intersect the range. Unit test seeds 6 monthly shards and asserts a 2-month query reads exactly 2 files.

2. **Root cause #2 fixed:** the post-`Execute` outcome-handling block (lines ~510-862) does not exit the loop on transient `Store.*` errors. Each `return result, err` in that block is converted to: log the error, emit a `kind:loop-error` bead event when possible, set the bead to a short cooldown, and `continue` to the next iteration. The only exception is context cancellation (`ctx.Err()`), which still terminates immediately.

3. **Regression tests for AC-2:** for each `Store.*` call in the outcome block (Unclaim, IncrNoChangesCount, adjudicateNoChanges, CloseWithEvidence on each path, Reopen on each path, SetExecutionCooldown on each path, late AppendEvent), a test injects a transient error from a fake store and asserts: (a) the loop continues, (b) a `kind:loop-error` event is recorded with the failing operation name, (c) `result.Failures++` advances.

4. **End-to-end reproduction guard:** an integration test seeds a bead.jsonl with 3 ready beads. The first returns `no_changes` (executor fake), the second returns `success`, the third returns `already_satisfied`. The loop runs to completion (all three processed) without exiting prematurely. With the pre-fix loop, this test fails on the first bead.

5. **Worker-state semantics preserved:** existing tests that assert worker.State transitions (`running` → `exited` / `failed` / `stopped` / `reaped`) still pass. The fix changes loop *advancement*, not terminal-state classification.

6. **Documented behavior:** `docs/agents/execute-loop.md` (or the closest existing doc) gains a "loop termination" section listing the only conditions that terminate the loop: context cancellation, `Once == true`, `PollInterval <= 0` AND `nextCandidate` returns no candidate, `RoutePreflight` rejected. Explicitly states that Store errors during outcome handling do NOT terminate the loop.

7. **Operator workaround retired:** the README's "if `ddx work` exits early, restart it" guidance (or equivalent) is removed; that workaround was masking this bug.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"1495ba706ffdc0e7bfb4833487ed068ffe732aeb","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:05:38.552407002Z","description":"## Symptom

`ddx work` (no `--once` flag, no `--poll-interval`, default mode) exits after processing exactly one bead instead of draining the queue. Reproduced four times today on `/home/erik/Projects/agent` with `--harness lmstudio` pinned (which sidesteps the unrelated `ddx-c7081f89` server-worker tier-walk issue):

| Run | Outcome on first bead | Worker terminal state | Queue still non-empty? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | `already_satisfied` (`agent-4aa3584e` adjudicated satisfied) | `already_satisfied` / `exited` | yes — 2 ready beads remained |
| 3 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |
| 4 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |
| 5 | `no_changes` from agent (`agent-6c7a4c11` declined as epic; bead left **claimed**) | `exited`, attempts=1 | yes — 5 ready beads remained |

In all four runs the loop should have continued to the next ready bead. Per `agent/.claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, only `success`/`already_satisfied` close the bead and `no_changes`/`land_conflict`/`*_failed` unclaim and continue. None of these outcomes is documented or expected to be loop-terminal.

## Root cause #1 (verified)

`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:382`:

```go
attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}

// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
    if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
        // ... log + emit ...
        continue
    }
}
```

The bead is added to `attempted` **before** the pre-claim hook runs. On hook failure (e.g. diverged branch), the loop `continue`s but the bead stays in `attempted`. Next iteration, `nextCandidate()` (line 924) skips the still-ready bead because it's in `attempted`, returns `ok=false`, and the loop exits at line 374 because `PollInterval <= 0`. Single-shot exit with `attempts=0`.

**Reproduces:** runs 3 and 4 above.

## Root cause #2 (suspected — needs targeted instrumentation)

Runs 2 and 5 reached `Execute()` and got a real report back. Worker log shows the entry line `▶ <bead>: <title>` but no closing `✓ <bead> → <result>` line (which is written at line 909). That means the loop exited somewhere between line 488 (`Executor.Execute`) and line 909 (the final summary log).

Between those bounds, **at least 16 distinct `return result, err` points** trigger if any `Store.*` operation returns an error: `Unclaim` (711, 700), `IncrNoChangesCount` (717), `adjudicateNoChanges` (721), `CloseWithEvidence` (520, 615, 694, 739), `Reopen` (635, 669), `SetExecutionCooldown` (749, 791, 803, 835, 844), late `AppendEvent` (862). Any single transient store error during outcome handling kills the worker.

For run 5 specifically, `agent-6c7a4c11` is now `Status: in_progress` with `Owner: ddx, Machine: sindri`. The bead was **never unclaimed** despite the agent returning `no_changes` — meaning the loop exited on or before line 711 (`w.Store.Unclaim`) failing. Investigation should look at what error `Unclaim` would return when called against a bead the same worker claimed seconds earlier; possible candidates: tracker-write race with concurrent worker, bead.jsonl re-write during a fetch by the pre-claim hook on a later iteration that already happened (no — pre-claim is pre-claim, not post-execute), or a malformed bead row that fails store-side validation.

Independent of the specific store error: the structural pattern of "any Store.* error → exit the entire loop" is the wrong default. A store error on outcome handling should at most park the bead and continue to the next, never terminate the worker.

## Why this is loadbearing

The loop is the queue-drain primitive. Every observed failure-to-drain in the last 24 hours of operator complaints traces back to this — beads that should have been the worker's second/third/fourth attempt never got picked up because the worker exited after one attempt, and the operator manually restarts the drain (sometimes after a long delay). The visible queue depth is misleading: half the "ready" beads are sitting behind a worker that quietly died.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — fixes for both root causes
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go` — regression tests for each path

## Out of scope

- Anything in the `agent` SDK (`/home/erik/Projects/agent`). The loop and all its store interactions are owned by ddx.
- The pre-claim hook's divergence-detection logic itself — that's correct behavior; the bug is how the loop reacts to the hook's signal.
- Adaptive min-tier / tier-walk machinery (`ddx-c7081f89`, `ddx-aec9d68c`) — independent.

## Anti-goals

- Do **not** swallow store errors silently. They must be surfaced (event + log) but should not kill the worker on transient/per-bead failures.
- Do **not** convert the loop's Once/PollInterval semantics — they're correct.
- Do **not** change worker terminal-state semantics (`exited`/`failed`/`stopped`/`reaped`).","events_attachment":"ddx-37cdb43a/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-37cdb43a","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execute-loop","kind:bug","phase:fix"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK

3. Token-budget guard: a unit test asserts that when a `<bead-review>` prompt would exceed 150k tokens even after the exclusion, the builder elides to a summary stub (per suggested fix #2) and logs a warning rather than emitting the full content.

4. 5. 6. Existing execution evidence continues to satisfy auditability: either still tracked but excluded from review diffs, or moved to a dedicated ref / ignored store per the chosen fix. `ddx agent metrics` and `ddx bead metrics` still surface per-attempt usage/tokens/cost from the evidence bundle after the fix.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T00:13:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1551897","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:08:23.667089738Z","description":"## Summary

On retry, DDx synthesizes a <bead-review> prompt whose <diff> section is the output of `git show <resultRev>`. When the prior attempt's landing sequence appended an evidence commit of the form `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt_id>]` that added `.ddx/executions/<attempt_id>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl` (full per-event NDJSON session log) as a tracked file, `resultRev` is that evidence commit's SHA (because `landEvidence` sets `result.NewTip = sha` and the orchestrator copies `land.NewTip` into `res.ResultRev`). `git show` of the evidence commit emits the entire session log diff with a `+` prefix per line. Session logs are 5-50k lines; the retry prompt exceeds every public context window.

## Category / severity / surface

- Category: correctness / resource-exhaustion
- Severity: high
- Surface: `ddx agent execute-bead` retry path triggered by `kind:review-malfunction` (and any other flow that re-synthesizes a <bead-review> prompt from a git diff/show since base_rev).

## Full bug description (verbatim)

On retry, DDx synthesizes a <bead-review> prompt whose <diff> section is the raw `git diff <base_rev>..<result_rev>` of the prior attempt. The prior attempt's landing sequence includes an evidence commit of the form:

    chore: add execution evidence [<attempt_id>]

that adds `.ddx/executions/<attempt_id>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl` — the full per-event NDJSON session log of the prior attempt — as a tracked file. Because that file is *added* in the diff range, the diff includes it in full with a `+` prefix on every line. Session logs are routinely 5-50k lines. One reasonable fix iteration is enough to push the retry prompt past 1-2 million tokens, which no currently supported model can accept — the provider streams back an n_keep > n_ctx error and the retry crashes without a request round-trip.

This is a self-poisoning loop: the "evidence" DDx commits to make attempts auditable is the exact artifact that makes the next attempt undeliverable.

## Reproduction (observed live 2026-04-17 in nexiq)

1. Bead `hx-dd4ab35f` claimed by execute-loop worker (harness=agent, model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b, provider=vidar).
2. Attempt `20260417T211600-d6a7dee6` succeeded and landed two commits: `cb5e733` (implementation + regression test [hx-dd4ab35f]) and `9a006dd` (chore: add execution evidence [20260417T211600-], adds `.ddx/executions/20260417T211600-d6a7dee6/embedded/agent-1776460560227794836.jsonl`, +20,623 lines).
3. Post-merge review emitted `BLOCK` without a rationale; DDx recorded `kind:review-malfunction` and reopened the bead for retry.
4. Retry `20260417T212928-27f9054a` synthesized `.ddx/executions/20260417T212928-27f9054a/prompt.md` of 6,307,997 bytes; `<bead-review>` block = 6,307,969 bytes; 99.9998% is the diff of the prior evidence commit, 99%+ of that diff is the session log file. Rest of <bead-review> (title/description/acceptance/labels/governing) is ~28 bytes.
5. lmstudio responded: "n_keep: 2,274,074 >= n_ctx: 196,608". Worker recorded failed attempt with zero usage. Same pattern on vidar (n_keep: 1,169,147) and bragi (n_keep: 2,274,074), all running qwen3.6-35b-a3b at max_context_length 262144. Provider/model is irrelevant — prompt exceeds every public context window.

## DDx code-path verification (Claude investigation 2026-04-17)

Relevant files and line ranges in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx:

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:553-561` — `DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow(rev)` runs `git -C <projectRoot> show <rev>` with no pathspec. Output is injected raw into `<diff>` by `BuildReviewPrompt` at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:383-384`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:427-431` — reviewer fetches `diff := r.gitShow(resultRev)` then builds the prompt.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:540-555` (`landEvidence`) — creates `chore: add execution evidence [<short>]` and sets `result.NewTip = sha`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:428-434` — `RealLandingGitOps.StageDir` runs `git add -- <relPath>` on the entire `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` directory with no exclusions for `embedded/`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:250-251` — after Land, `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`, so `ResultRev` ends up pointing at the evidence commit. The reviewer then runs `git show <evidence-commit>`, which is literally the dump of the session log.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1135-1164` (`VerifyCleanWorktree`) — also stages the evidence dir via `git add -- <evidenceDir>` and commits `chore: add execution evidence [<short>]`; same no-exclusion pattern.
- `cli/cmd/bead_review.go:162-171` (`beadReviewGitShow`) — mirror of the same bug in the standalone `ddx bead review` command.

Confirmation the bug reporter's claim about `:(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded`:
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:269-283` (`SynthesizeCommit`) — the exclusion pathspec IS present here; the bug is that the evidence-landing commit path (`StageDir` + `VerifyCleanWorktree`) and the review path (`git show <rev>`) do not share this exclusion.
- `cli/cmd/init.go:19-46` — the gitignore rules DO include `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/`, but `git add -- <dir>` on pre-existing or explicitly-added paths can still stage, and older workspaces may pre-date this gitignore (matches nexiq's scenario where the evidence commit landed the session log).

## Deviation from bug description (minor)

The bug text describes the diff as `git diff <base_rev>..<result_rev>`. The code actually does `git show <resultRev>` (single-rev show). The self-poisoning mechanism is functionally identical: when `resultRev` points at the evidence commit (`landEvidence` + `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`), `git show` emits the entire `+`-prefixed session log. All suggested fixes still apply.

## Suggested fixes (ascending scope)

1. Exclude execution-evidence paths from bead-review diffs by passing a pathspec to the `git show`/`git diff` call in the prompt builder: `-- . ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded/*' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/usage.json'`. The same exclusion already exists at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:269-283` for `SynthesizeCommit`; replicate it in `DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow` (`execute_bead_review.go:553-561`), `beadReviewGitShow` (`cli/cmd/bead_review.go:162-171`), `landEvidence` → `StageDir` (`execute_bead_land.go:428-434,540-555`), and `VerifyCleanWorktree` (`execute_bead.go:1135-1164`).
2. Substitute, don't reproduce. When a tracked-file addition exceeds a byte/line budget in the diff, replace it with a one-line stub such as: `+++ b/.ddx/executions/.../agent-*.jsonl  (+20,623 lines of session evidence — omitted from review prompt)`.
3. Stop committing raw session logs as tracked evidence. Move `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/*.jsonl` to a gitignored store, or a separate `refs/ddx/evidence/*` ref analogous to `refs/ddx/iterations/*`.
4. Cap the <bead-review> prompt size with a hard budget (e.g., 150k tokens). Elide to summary + log warning when exceeded.

(1) is the minimal surgical fix; (3) is the correct long-term fix.

## Evidence in nexiq repo

- Bloated retry prompt: `.ddx/executions/20260417T212928-27f9054a/prompt.md` (6,307,997 bytes; <bead-review> body = 6,307,969 bytes).

The bulk of this unification **already shipped** as `ddx-87fb72c2` (epic `ddx-fdd3ea36`) before this epic was filed:

- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` and `agent.routing.model_overrides` are now opt-in (consulted only with `--escalate` / `--override-model`).
- The default execute path on the CLI (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`) calls `ResolveRoute` once per attempt instead of iterating a tier ladder.
- `agent.routing.default_harness` was removed (hard error if still present).
- `agent.ResolveTierCandidates` (from `ddx-5538aa5b`) replaced the broken literal-tier-name-as-Model pattern; tier resolution now consults the catalog.
- See `docs/migrations/routing-config.md` for the migration the user-facing CLI surface follows.

**Remaining gap (this epic now scopes only this):** the server worker path in `cli/internal/server/workers.go:650` was not updated by `ddx-87fb72c2`. It still derives `escalationEnabled := spec.Harness == "" && spec.Model == ""` instead of mirroring the CLI's `escalate && harness == "" && model == ""`. That mismatch is why `ddx work` (default mode, server-submit) still hits "all tiers exhausted — no viable provider" with `model_overrides` pointing at non-served models, while `ddx work --local --no-adaptive-min-tier` succeeds. Verified live in this session: `worker-20260429T033325-bedf` failed; `--no-adaptive-min-tier` got `ddx-98e6e9ef` to merge at `115b47c0` on local-tier qwen for zero cost.

Sole child: `ddx-c7081f89`. Once that lands, this epic closes.

## Strategic direction (preserved for future reference)

DDx embeds the agent SDK as a Go library to produce traceable execution units: `(bead, input_state) → (output_state, processing_metadata)`. DDx owns the execution unit and its metadata (bead events, cost ledger, escalation summary, evidence directories), and it owns operator policy (`--harness`/`--model`/`--profile`/`--max-cost`/`--no-review`/`--escalate`). It does **not** need to own provider/harness/cost/local-first routing — agent SDK already does that.

| Concern | DDx (now, post-87fb72c2) | Agent SDK |
|---|---|---|
| Profile selection | `RouteRequest.Profile` straight to agent | `default/cheap/standard/smart/local/offline/air-gapped/fast/code-smart/code-high` |
| Per-tier model pinning | opt-in via `--override-model` | `RouteRequest.Model` |
| Escalation on no-provider | opt-in via `--escalate` (CLI path); **not yet on server worker path** | `escalateProfileLadder` walks `ProfileEscalationLadder=[cheap,standard,smart]` on `ErrNoLiveProvider` |
| Cost-aware ranking | n/a (delegated) | `internal/routing/score.go` ranks by `CostClass` (local=0 wins) |
| Adaptive success-rate gating | `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` (sticky 50-window cheap-tier lockout, only consumed via `--escalate`) | `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess map[harness]float64` |
| Decision trace | bead events `kind:tier-attempt` per RouteCandidate | `RouteDecision.Candidates []RouteCandidate` |

## Optional follow-ups (not in this epic — file separately if/when needed)

These are no longer blockers; track separately if pursued:

- **Default profile = `local`** so the operator-default honors the local-LLM-first goal without an explicit `--profile local`. Currently default is `default` which maps via project config.
- **Inject `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess` per-harness** to replace `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` with agent-native success-rate routing. Frees DDx from maintaining its own success-window state machine.
- **Delete `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go` helpers** once no caller (CLI or server) consumes them. Currently consumed only via `--escalate`; if `--escalate` is dropped or moved into agent, these can go.
- **Delete `cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go::AdaptiveMinTier`** once the HistoricalSuccess injection above is in place.

## Out of scope for this epic

- Changes to agent SDK source — if a gap is found, file an upstream bead under `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.
- UI/e2e work — the parent e2e drift epic `ddx-ccdf9cf9` covers that and is independent.
- Server source changes outside `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — no API endpoint changes, no GraphQL schema changes.","events_attachment":"ddx-3f406266/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3f406266","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:routing","area:agent","kind:refactor","phase:build"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK

Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"3967690","closing_commit_sha":"cedf316fc6d5082eff11bff2f38ee5618958b20f","created_at":"2026-04-30T00:10:22.985451193Z","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/<id>/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.

Concrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):

  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)
  Harness: claude / model: sonnet
  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls
  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit
  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,
    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts
  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —
    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)
    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)
  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of model time spent making them.

The same pattern appeared on:
  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)
  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)
  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)
  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)

Total burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.

The execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:
  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add <specific-paths>...'

So agent self-commit is the contract. But:

(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the harness sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.

(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.

The issue spans multiple harnesses (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one provider's commit semantics. Either:
  - The harness should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration
  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language
  - The harness should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as harness-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25.451101407Z","id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:harness","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:43:46.370829758Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:383:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing what my agents have done, I open the Executions view, filter to a specific bead or verdict or harness, click into one, and see the prompt it received, the verdict it produced, the session it corresponds to (with cost + tokens), and every tool call it made — all without leaving the browser.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Sidebar entry.** "Executions" added to the project sidebar between "Sessions" and "Personas" (or wherever fits the existing visual order — design call; captured in the PR description). Active-highlight works.

2. **List route.** `/executions` renders a paginated, filterable table with columns: timestamp, bead (id + title as link), harness/model, verdict (color-coded pass/block/error/other), duration, cost. Default sort: newest first. Filters: verdict, harness, bead, since/until date, free-text search over prompt first ~200 chars.

3. **Detail route.** `/executions/<id>` shows five tabs as described (Manifest, Prompt, Result, Session, Tool calls). Tab switching doesn't re-fetch already-loaded data.

4. **Tool calls pane.** Virtualized list. First 50 calls load with the page; subsequent pages load on scroll. Each entry shows tool name + collapsed input/output, expandable on click. Links to the raw agent-log file for operators who want to diff.

5. **GraphQL completeness.** `executions`, `execution`, `executionToolCalls` resolvers exist and pass feat-008-style integration tests. Schema documented in `schema.graphql`.

6. **Cross-links present.**
   - Bead detail renders an "Executions" section listing linked executions with verdict + timestamp, each clicking into the detail route.
   - Session detail shows "Execution" link when the session has one.
   - Commits page shows execution link when commit sha == execution.result_rev.

7. **Fixture + perf.**
   - Seeded fixture: ≥1,000 executions, ≥100 with seeded tool-call streams (≥50 calls each).
   - List view on this fixture: p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Detail view (no tool calls): p95 ≤ 150ms HTTP.
   - Tool-call page 1 (first 50 entries): p95 ≤ 300ms HTTP.
   - If any target cannot be met in-place, raise DB-substrate decision with user before switching.

8. **Playwright e2e.**
   - Seeds a project with 3 executions across 2 beads with mixed verdicts.
   - Navigates to `/executions`, asserts the table renders 3 rows and filter-by-verdict narrows correctly.
   - Clicks into one execution, asserts all 5 tabs exist and the Prompt tab shows the seeded prompt.
   - Opens the Tool calls tab, expands an entry, asserts input/output rendering.
   - Clicks the linked bead from the detail, asserts navigation to bead detail and that the Executions section on that bead lists this execution.

9. **Depends on.**
   - Soft dependency on `ddx-2ceb02fa` for the Session tab's cost/token numbers. If not yet landed, render "not available" for those fields and wire them in after.
   - Adjacent: `ddx-6904a90b` (billing mode) — Session tab's cost shows the correct bucket once that lands.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"59a4ece5923fa29a89addac195937780c28ec955","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:55:34.427486388Z","description":"## Observed / Motivation

`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json}` bundles are already being produced per `ddx agent execute-bead` attempt — 469 of them on the current repo. They contain the canonical record of what an agent was asked to do and what verdict the attempt produced. **Nothing in the web UI surfaces them today.** A user who wants to ask "why did this bead end up in this state?" has to drop to the filesystem.

Sessions (`sessions.jsonl` / sharded index per `ddx-2ceb02fa`) carry the harness/cost/token telemetry but not the verdict and not the bead linkage. Agent log streams (`agent-*.jsonl`) carry the tool-call trace but are scattered across hundreds of files. **Executions are the natural join** — they connect a bead, a session, a prompt, a verdict, and a tool-call stream.

## Ground truth

Per-execution on disk:

```
.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/
  manifest.json      { harness, model, base_rev, result_rev, verdict, bead_id, execution_dir }
  prompt.md          the prompt that was sent
  result.json        { verdict, rationale, ... }
```

Relations:
- `manifest.bead_id` → bead
- a session id is recorded in bead events (`kind:cost`, `kind:routing`) and in the agent-log stream; both can map back to an execution via correlation metadata
- tool calls live in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` (and `agent-s-*.jsonl`), keyed by session id

## Scope

### Part 1 — GraphQL surface

- `executions(projectId, first, after, beadId?, since?, until?, verdict?, harness?)` — paginated list of execution bundles.
- `execution(id)` — one bundle, fully materialized: manifest fields, prompt markdown, result JSON, linked bead (id + title), linked session (id + harness + model + cost + token totals), tool-call stream pointer.
- `executionToolCalls(id, first, after)` — paginated tool-call stream for the execution, read lazily from the agent-log file. Matches the event shape used by the workers detail page.

Executions source of truth: directory scan of `.ddx/executions/` filtered/paginated at the resolver. Shard discipline: follow the sessions-index monthly shard pattern (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) if listing all executions grows beyond tens of thousands — per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`, raise the storage decision with the user before switching substrate.

### Part 2 — Sidebar + routes

- Add "Executions" to the project sidebar (`NavShell.svelte`).
- Routes:
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions` — list view with filters (verdict, harness, bead, date range, free-text over prompt). Table columns: timestamp, bead, harness/model, verdict (pass/block/error), duration, cost.
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions/<id>` — detail view with five tabs: **Manifest**, **Prompt** (rendered markdown), **Result** (rationale + raw JSON toggle), **Session** (harness, model, cost, token totals, link to `/sessions/<id>`), **Tool calls** (virtualized lazy-scroll list of tool_use + tool_result pairs with expandable payloads).

### Part 3 — Cross-links (make the graph real)

- **Bead detail:** new "Executions" section listing all executions for this bead with verdict + timestamp + link. Already have data: manifest.bead_id.
- **Session detail:** new "Execution" link pointing to the execution this session ran for, when the session has a linked execution.
- **Commits page:** already shows commits; if a commit was produced by an execution (result_rev == commit sha), surface the execution link.
- **Graph/Docs pages:** out of scope for this bead.

### Part 4 — Perf

- Fixture: ≥1,000 executions, per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`. Tool-call streams seeded for 10% of them.
- Targets: list view p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP, detail view without tool calls p95 ≤ 150ms HTTP, tool-call page-1 (first 50) p95 ≤ 300ms HTTP.
- If these can't be met with filesystem scan + in-memory filter, raise DB-substrate decision with user per standing directive.

## Out of scope

- Writing new execution bundles (that's already happening).
- Executions from other projects/nodes (keep per-project for v1).
- Tool-call payload redaction / privacy. File a follow-up if relevant.
- `.claude/worktrees/` worktree lifecycle tracking — covered by companion bead per user's split request.","events_attachment":"ddx-726bc5f2/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model "minimax/minimax-m2.7" for harness "gemini"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-726bc5f2","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","feat-006","ui","executions"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:447:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator using a browser — on a machine without a terminal, or a phone, or a laptop away from my dev box — I can open my project's home page, type a prompt into a chat pane, and see the DDx agent work on it in real time. Tool calls stream as they happen, costs accrue visibly, and the conversation persists so I can come back to it.

**Acceptance criteria (written for Option A; rewrite if design note chooses B/C/D):**

1. **Design note landed.** One-pager in `docs/helix/02-design/` records the chosen option, the answers to the six subsidiary questions, and the message/thread schema. Implementation PR links to it.

2. **Thread model.** Chat threads persist under `.ddx/chat/<thread-id>/{manifest.json,messages.jsonl}`. One thread per project per operator by default; operators can create named threads. Messages.jsonl is append-only, one JSON object per message (user or assistant), with `sessionId` linking each assistant turn back to an entry in the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`).

3. **GraphQL surface.** New resolvers:
   - Query: `chatThreads(projectId)`, `chatThread(id)`, `chatMessages(threadId, since?)`.
   - Mutation: `chatThreadCreate(projectId, title?)`, `chatSend(threadId, message, harness?, profile?)` → kicks off a session via `service.Execute`.
   - Subscription: `chatStream(threadId)` → emits assistant message chunks + tool call events (same event vocabulary as `workers.recentEvents`).

4. **Home page UI.** Project home page gains a chat pane below the actions section. Pane shows thread list, active thread messages, input box, harness/profile picker. Tool calls render inline within assistant messages. Streaming updates as chunks arrive.

5. **Session linkage.** Each assistant turn creates one session in the sessions index. Clicking an assistant message shows the session id and a link to the session detail (`/sessions/<id>`). Chat thread header shows cumulative session count + total cost for the thread.

6. **Concurrency.** Two browsers open on the same thread see each other's messages within 2s (subscription-driven). Two operators on different threads in the same project do not interfere.

7. **Interrupt.** The in-flight assistant turn can be cancelled via a Stop button; server sends the harness the equivalent of `ddx agent workers stop <id>` for the in-flight session. The thread records a cancelled turn, does not try to auto-resume.

8. **Cost guardrail (soft).** If the cumulative thread cost passes a configurable ceiling (`.ddx/config.yaml: chat.cost_ceiling_usd`, default unset), the UI warns but does not block. Hard block is a follow-up.

9. **Auth posture.**
   - Per design note. If the chosen posture is "single-operator localhost / tsnet only for v1," the chat routes are served unauthenticated behind tsnet and the design note records the gap to close before broader deployment.
   - A small middleware gate is in place even for v1 so adding auth later is schema-local, not route-local.

10. **Playwright e2e.**
    - Seeds a project with zero chat threads.
    - Navigates to project home, asserts the chat pane renders with "Start a new thread" affordance.
    - Starts a thread, sends a short prompt, simulates a streamed session response with one tool call, asserts chunks appear in order and the session id link is present.
    - Opens a second browser context on the same thread, asserts both see new messages within 2s.
    - Cancels an in-flight turn, asserts the cancelled state is persisted.

11. **Depends on.**
    - `ddx-2513f08c` (home page reachable from sidebar) — hard dependency.
    - `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) — hard dependency for the session linkage + cost rollup.

12. **Explicitly deferred.** PTY integration (Option B / pop-out terminal from Option C) is its own bead, filed only if the Option A rollout surfaces real interactive-steering needs.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"9c29b5fe9cb5e06c3a0f1ca3d8133f8766b59259","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:51:38.374211227Z","description":"## Observed / Motivation

User wants to "lift work out of the TUI entirely and allow non-tmux users to work remotely." Today the only way to prompt the DDx agent stack interactively is a terminal session. The web UI is read-mostly: dispatch three actions (drain/align/checks) and review results. There is no surface that lets an operator say "go do X" against the project in natural language from a browser.

Related UX: the project home page already exists (`/nodes/.../projects/<id>/`), renders queue summary + actions, and is the natural host for a chat affordance. First the home page needs to be reachable from the sidebar (`ddx-2513f08c`) — this bead depends on that work.

## The tension

The DDx harness abstraction (`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go`, `service.Execute`) is designed for **non-interactive** use: prompt in, structured result out, optional streaming. This is the right shape for queue-draining, quorum, evaluation, and automation. It is **not** the shape of a TUI conversation with tab completion, mid-run steering, and back-channel signals.

An operator-facing chat can land on either side of that line. The bead records all four options so the design phase can pick one deliberately.

## Options (design phase decides, recommended path noted)

### Option A — Chat over the harness abstraction (RECOMMENDED starting point)

- Chat UI posts a prompt → server dispatches a session via `service.Execute` (or a wrapper) → streams tool calls + response chunks back over a GraphQL subscription.
- Message history is persisted as a thread; each user turn = one new session. Responses reuse the existing `recentEvents` stream shape already used on the workers detail page.
- Works with every harness today because the harness abstraction is what it is. claude, codex, agent (embedded) all participate.
- **Trade-off:** no mid-run steering, no tab completion, no live back-and-forth inside one agent invocation.
- Matches the user's stated target audience (non-TUI users who want remote browser access). Those users aren't trying to steer a TUI — they're trying to leave it behind.

### Option B — PTY gateway per harness

- Server owns a PTY pool. Each chat thread attaches to one harness process (claude/codex/etc.) over a WebSocket; browser renders xterm.js.
- Full interactivity, including TUI steering.
- **Trade-off:** PTY lifecycle, auth, resource limits, tab-close-loses-session semantics, multi-user access control — all real. The harness abstraction is bypassed for this mode, which creates a second execution path to maintain.

### Option C — Hybrid (chat-first + pop-out terminal)

- Option A is the primary surface. When a chat reaches a point that needs interactive steering, a "Open terminal" escape hatch attaches a PTY to the current harness session.
- **Trade-off:** more surface area, but honest about both use cases. Most sessions stay on the cheap path.

### Option D — Web-tmux / ttyd wrapper

- Embed the existing terminal tooling directly: ttyd or xterm.js over a tmux socket owned by the server.
- **Trade-off:** fastest to ship, zero harness work, but the UX is a terminal in a browser — not "chat." Requires tmux. Doesn't serve the stated "non-tmux users" audience.

### Recommendation

Start with **A**. If the design note documents real interactive-steering needs we can't paper over, the right follow-up is **C** (A + PTY escape hatch), not B (PTY-only).

## Scope of this bead

- Design note in `docs/helix/02-design/` locking the option, the message schema, persistence model, and auth posture. Implementation PR links to it.
- First implementation pass: Option A.
- Home page gains a chat pane as its primary interaction surface, alongside the existing queue summary + actions (which stay — they're quick-dispatch affordances, not replaced by chat).

## Subsidiary questions the design note must answer

1. **Message persistence.** Are chat threads per-project or per-user? Stored where (`.ddx/chat/<thread-id>/` or elsewhere)? Is history shared across operators on the same project, or isolated per operator identity?
2. **Harness selection.** Does the chat pick a harness per-thread (picker in the UI) or per-message (re-route each turn)? Default to profile-based selection?
3. **Cost accountability.** Every chat message spawns a session. Sessions are already tracked in the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). The chat thread should surface cumulative cost to the operator so they don't blow budget unknowingly.
4. **Tool execution scope.** Chat prompts will trigger agent tool calls that read/write the project tree. Is the chat session bounded to the same permissions as `ddx agent run`? Does it run in the project worktree, a worktree per thread, a scratch dir? Default safe answer: same worktree as `ddx agent run` (user trusts ddx running anyway), but design note records it.
5. **Concurrency.** What happens if two operators have the chat open on the same project and both send messages? Default: independent threads, each with its own session — no shared state.
6. **Auth.** Today the server assumes single-operator localhost. Remote use implies some identity concept. Design note picks a posture: delegate to reverse proxy, ship a simple token, or punt and require tsnet + single-operator for v1.

## Out of scope

- Full PTY implementation (Option B). Follow-up bead if A+hybrid doesn't suffice.
- Cross-project chat.
- Voice input.","events_attachment":"ddx-88e91fdb/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-88e91fdb","issue_type":"epic","labels":["feat-008","feat-006","chat","remote-ops","design"],"priority":2,"session_id":"eb-45976f78","status":"closed","title":"Add chat interface to project home page: prompt ddx remotely without the TUI","updated_at":"2026-04-28T22:40:26.03095778Z"}

The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Harness, WorkerRecord.Model, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.

This bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.

## Required changes

### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)
Register alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:
  --json         emit raw JSON array
  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C
  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)

### 2. Data sources (two paths)
A. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.
B. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:
   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)
   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, harness, model, started_at

### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan
For each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:
  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)
  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)
  - Read execute-loop-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available
  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields

### 4. Table output (default)
Columns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | HARNESS | MODEL | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS
- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)
- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)
- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID

### 5. Merge and deduplicate
When both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.

## Key files
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()
- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)
- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","events_attachment":"ddx-9060c8b2/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T07:45:03.73510309Z","id":"ddx-9060c8b2","issue_type":"task","labels":["ux","execute-loop","observability","cli"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-3f16c49","status":"closed","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:47.499704451Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:521:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer using the web UI on a real-world-sized workspace (tens of projects × hundreds of beads each), the beads list opens in under a second and the cross-project all-beads view stays usable. Before any perf fix lands, we can reproduce the problem and measure improvement — not guess.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Perf harness exists and is reusable.**
   - Fixture builder generates `N × M` beads across projects with configurable shape (status mix, label density, dep count). Reuses the real `bead.Store` write path (no fake writer).
   - Benchmark covers at minimum: `bead(id:)`, `beadsByProject`, `beads` (cross-project), `docGraph`, `sessions`. Adding a new target is a single-table-row change.
   - Each target produces p50/p95/p99 for in-process calls and end-to-end HTTP. Results write to a human-readable report (markdown or JSON) plus a machine-readable artifact suitable for CI diffing later.

2. **Baseline recorded.** First run of the harness on the current `main` binary is stored in the bead notes and in `docs/helix/04-observe/perf/YYYY-MM-DD-baseline.md` (or equivalent existing location). Numbers include hardware + go version footer.

3. **BeadsByProject scope fix.** `BeadsByProject` calls a new `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, …)` that opens exactly one project's store. Unit test asserts only that project's store is opened (no iteration over other projects).

4. **Filter pushdown.** Status/label/projectID filters apply at the per-file / per-entry parse boundary, not post-materialization. Benchmark captures before/after for the common filtered case (e.g., `status=open`).

5. **Perf targets on fixture (10 projects × 500 beads = 5000 beads total):**
   - `bead(id:)` p95 ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP (already covered by `ddx-ad0db8fd`; this bead may inherit the work).
   - `beadsByProject(first=50)` p95 ≤ 75ms in-process / ≤ 250ms HTTP.
   - `beads(first=50, no projectID)` p95 ≤ 200ms in-process / ≤ 500ms HTTP.
   These are targets for the **first** pass (scope + pushdown). If they are not met with (1)+(2), escalate to the in-memory index (step 3 of Part 2 in the description) and update these targets with the new baseline.

6. **Stale-read safety.** Regardless of fix chosen, a test writes a bead via `ddx bead update`, then queries it via GraphQL, then asserts the new value is visible on the very next request. Works under concurrency: N concurrent readers + writers interleaved without torn reads or panics.

7. **CI posture (soft).** The harness is runnable via `make bench-graphql` (or the project's equivalent). CI wiring to fail on >25% regression is a follow-up bead — recorded in this bead's notes with the proposed threshold but not implemented here.

8. **Playwright smoke.** Opening `/beads` on the fixture project returns an interactive (clickable) list within 1s. Opening the cross-project `/beads` page returns within 2s. These are ceilings, not p95 targets — guards against outright regression, not fine-grained perf.

9. **Cross-reference.** This bead's notes list `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions shard/index) as the other two bead/session perf threads that should inherit this harness.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"7a2078a59d9885cb94f4c188ea95ce5149f969cd","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:41:52.271330549Z","description":"## Observed

Per-project `/beads` page is noticeably slow; the cross-project "all beads" view is substantially slower. Related: `ddx-ad0db8fd` already identified the single-bead-detail slowness caused by the same storage pattern.

## Root cause (same pattern, different call site)

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go:22,40` —

- `Beads` (cross-project) and `BeadsByProject` (single-project) both call `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots(status, label, projectID, search)`.
- `GetBeadSnapshots` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105`) iterates every registered project, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), filters in Go. Cost is `O(projects × beads_per_project)` on every call, independent of the filter/page size.
- All filtering (status, label, project, search) is Go-side, post-read. The page-size window is applied in `beadConnectionFromSnapshots` after the full corpus is already materialized.
- Cross-project "all beads" = same loop, just without the projectID filter. It is the worst case by design.

This is the same shape that's breaking the Sessions feed (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) and single-bead detail (`ddx-ad0db8fd`). A point fix here is insufficient — we need a measurement surface first so we stop relying on anecdote.

## Proposed direction

### Part 1 — Perf harness (foundational; enables everything else)

Introduce a reusable GraphQL perf harness under `cli/internal/server/perf/` or `cli/internal/server/graphql/bench_test.go`:

- Seeded fixture builder: generates N projects × M beads with configurable distribution of status/labels/dependencies. Reuses existing store primitives (no fake data layer).
- Go benchmark + scriptable HTTP runner (wrapper around an already-running server) reporting p50/p95/p99 per query shape.
- Targets covered on day one: `bead(id:)`, `beadsByProject(projectID, first, after, status, label, search)`, `beads(projectID=nil, first, after, status, label)`, `docGraph`, `sessions` (so the sessions shard work `ddx-2ceb02fa` inherits this harness). New query targets are added by writing a single table row.
- Thresholds are **recorded, not asserted**, in the first pass. CI surfaces a regression if a threshold is breached beyond a configured delta (e.g., +25% p95). Initial pass is "land the harness and record baseline"; a follow-up wires it into CI.

### Part 2 — Fix the scan pattern

Minimum viable, in priority order:

1. **Scope by project when the query is scoped.** `BeadsByProject` should call a new `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, filters…)` that only opens that one project's store. Trivial change; eliminates the cross-project factor for the common case.
2. **Early filter pushdown.** `GetBeadSnapshots` should apply status/label/projectID filters at the file-parse boundary where cheap, not after materializing every bead. (Still reads every file — deeper fix below.)
3. **Lightweight in-memory index with invalidation.** After (1) and (2), if "all beads" or search is still slow, introduce an index keyed `{projectID, beadID}` with basic metadata (status, labels, title, updatedAt). Refresh on bead write + debounced filesystem watch. Full-body reads stay lazy — the index never holds bodies. This is the same storage discipline we adopted for sessions in `ddx-2ceb02fa`.

The harness from Part 1 is the gate for choosing between (2) and (3); don't commit to the index without numbers.

## Out of scope

- Full-text search infrastructure (tracked separately if needed).
- Changing the on-disk bead format.
- Cross-node federation performance. Current concern is a single server reading a single workspace.","events_attachment":"ddx-9ce6842a/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"stalled: read_only_tools_exceeded","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-23T10:04:40Z","feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-9ce6842a","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-004","perf","graphql"],"notes":"## 2026-04-22 — User directives on fixture size and storage strategy

Two standing instructions from the user that apply to this bead and to all other perf beads (`ddx-ad0db8fd`, `ddx-2ceb02fa`, and any spawned from efficacy/providers work):

1. **Minimum fixture size: 1,000s of records.** The original acceptance criterion (10 projects × 500 beads = 5,000) already meets this for beads, but restate the floor explicitly: **no perf work lands against a fixture smaller than 1,000 records per query shape**. Sessions fixtures, efficacy fixtures, provider/quota history fixtures — all ≥1k rows. Beads fixture stays at 5k; sessions fixture for `ddx-2ceb02fa` should be ≥10k index entries (already specified); efficacy and any new surfaces ≥1k attempts/rows each.

2. **DB backend is on the table.** If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, lightweight index) cannot hit the target latencies on the fixture sizes above, the user is open to introducing a real data store — candidates named: **axon**, **sqlite**, **postgres**. Do not silently pick one: **raise the decision with the user before choosing a path.** Capture the baseline, capture what in-place optimization achieved, then bring the choice (and the rationale) to the user.

These directives supersede any acceptance criterion that implies "optimize in place no matter what." If the numbers say storage needs to change, that's the outcome — but the user decides the substrate.

**AC §8 — Playwright cross-project test routes wrong path**: `beads-smoke.spec.ts:107` navigates to `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/projects/${PROJECT_ID}/beads` for the "cross-project" test, which is the same per-project URL as the 1s test. `ddx agent execute-loop` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.
2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.
3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.
4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.

## Observed gap
`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.

## Why this matters
Open epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. Launching them directly makes queue-drain behavior unstable and can strand the loop on non-executable work even when bounded tasks are ready behind the epic.","events_attachment":"ddx-b44ca394/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T03:39:25.89063588Z","execute-loop-last-detail":"agent execution failed","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-13T21:32:10Z","execution-eligible":true,"id":"ddx-b44ca394","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","area:agent","area:bead","area:cli"],"notes":"Blocker ddx-cf340665 is closed.
